summary: Frontend-only E2E testing of SlotSync (Streamlit) — home hero+stats+navbar, search filter, course expander, faculty dropdowns, clash detection, add/update, Selected tab, Timetable HTML grid, Auto-Generate options, and PDF export were all tested end-to-end via Playwright. All 13 requested feature areas are functionally working. PDF export returns a valid application/pdf (%PDF magic verified, 4092 bytes, correct filename).

issues: Streamlit st.text_input requires Enter/blur to apply — 'Press Enter to apply' badge appears while typing. Spec said 'filters instantly'; current behaviour is on-Enter/blur which is standard Streamlit UX. Minor UX gap only.

When two selected courses share a slot (user overrides a clash), only one course is rendered in the shared cell — no visual overlap indicator. Clash was already flagged during add, so this is acceptable but could show a small warning.

critical_code_review_comments: utils/style.py is 352 lines of CSS in Python — fine but consider extracting to a .css file loaded once.

components/search.py: default_idx match uses `already.get('faculty_theory', already.get('faculty', ''))` which can be a concatenated 'A · B' string for Theory+Lab; matching may fail on re-edit. Low priority.

data.py `num_faculty` counts unique faculty names (33), not rows (48). Label 'Faculty Loaded' could be clarified as unique instructors vs offerings — not a bug, just a naming nuance from the review request wording.
counter.py silently ignores network failures and falls back to a local JSON, which is correct; ensure /app/data/visitor_count.json is git-ignored to avoid spurious commits.
seed_data_creation: None. Used existing /app/data/courses.csv and /app/data/faculty.csv. Feature request pair (AI A1+TA1 vs DSA A1+TA1) verified for clash.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Streamlit expanders are native <details>. To reliably click buttons INSIDE expanders via Playwright: click details summary NATIVELY first (`page.locator('details summary').first.click()`), then use `await add_btn.evaluate('el => el.click()')` for the Add Course button. Using `evaluate('el.open=true')` + Playwright force click DOES NOT register the button click on the Streamlit server (state doesn't mutate). This wasted 2 attempts before finding the workaround. Also Streamlit st.text_input requires .press('Enter') to apply. Sample clash pair for testing: 'Artificial Intelligence' A1+TA1 (Monali Bordoloi) vs 'Data Structures and Algorithms' A1+TA1 (Deepashika Mishra). PDF download button is `[data-testid="stDownloadButton"] button` (native download event fires via page.expect_download).
rca of the issue: No app bug. Test-only friction: Playwright force-click on a button inside a programmatically-opened <details> element does not trigger Streamlit's server-side widget event. Root cause is that Streamlit binds click handlers on the React component tree; force-click bypasses element-visibility gates but the details ancestor still intercepts and the event doesn't reach the React handler with the right dispatch context. Native JS `.click()` via `evaluate` works. Reproduction: expand details via el.open=true, force-click Add Course — no state change. Mitigation used above: click summary natively, then el.click() on button.
